title: lichen_lora_l2_stop: rx_thread_data reuse after join may race with double-start
description: In lora_l2.c, lichen_lora_l2_stop() calls k_thread_join(&rx_thread_data) at line 263, and lichen_lora_l2_start() calls k_thread_create() on the same static rx_thread_data at line 235. After stop() completes, calling start() again reuses rx_thread_data. However, if stop() times out on join (RX_TIMEOUT_MS + 1000), the thread may still be winding down while start() reinitializes rx_thread_data, causing undefined behavior. The k_thread_join() return value is not checked - if it returns non-zero (e.g., -EAGAIN on timeout), the subsequent start() corrupts an active thread.
